name: Emily Cain, EMILY's List
subject: Marches + protests ➡ Electing women
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5637/24c69fd9/11bb1dbe3/18942b9c/4073006378/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFRN25N_digital_email_20180213_StrongNumbers_inact&source=WQEFRN25N_digital_email_20180213_StrongNumbers_inact")
---

Peter,

One of the biggest battles our candidates will face this year is already here: primaries.

For the 10 House candidates we've endorsed with competitive primaries next month, this is a make-or-break moment that determines if they will even be on the ballot in November.

This is our last Membership Week before those primaries. If we can put up strong numbers this week, we will be able to make sure our women have the support they need to win.

**[Donate $3 now to become a member for 2018 and help our women make it through their primaries next month.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5637/24c69fd9/11bb1dbe3/18942b9d/4073006378/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180212_email_FebMembershipWeek?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+if+consinConsGroup9050+now+to+ren&utm_campaign=WQEFRN25N_digital_email_20180213_StrongNumbers_inact&source=WQEFRN25N_digital_email_20180213_StrongNumbers_inact")**

Primaries begin as early as March 6th in Texas and March 20th in Illinois.

As the first primaries this year, they will be a key test to see if the resistance of 2017 will translate into wins in 2018.

Last year's marches and protests were not a flash in the pan. They were the beginning of a movement that can sweep the next generation of pro-choice Democratic women into office.

But we have to start this election year off strong by gathering the resources our candidates need to win these primaries to make that a reality.

**Donate $3 now to become a member for 2018 and help our women win their primaries:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/February-2018-Membership-Week](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5637/24c69fd9/11bb1dbe3/18942b9e/4073006378/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180212_email_FebMembershipWeek?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgFebruary2018Memb&utm_campaign=WQEFRN25N_digital_email_20180213_StrongNumbers_inact&source=WQEFRN25N_digital_email_20180213_StrongNumbers_inact")**

Thanks -- and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

**[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c5637/24c69fd9/11bb1dbe3/18942b9f/4073006378/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180212_email_FebMembershipWeek?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFRN25N_digital_email_20180213_StrongNumbers_inact&source=WQEFRN25N_digital_email_20180213_StrongNumbers_inact")**

subject: It's a great day to celebrate women

Peter,

Galentine's Day is all about celebrating women. It's a day set aside specifically to let the women in your life know just how great they are and how much you appreciate them.

And even though at EMILY's List we celebrate women every day by working to elect them, today we're taking a moment to do something a little bit more special for all of the women in our community.

**Text GAL to 47717 for a Galentine's Day surprise you don't want to miss!**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Galentines-Day-Surprise](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c562c/24c69fd9/11a2fc8fc/1894228a/1155629835/VEsE/ "https://elistlive.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/20180213_email_SMSGalentine?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgGalentinesDaySur&utm_campaign=WQENEN26N_digital_email_20180213_SMSGalentine_inact&source=WQENEN26N_digital_email_20180213_SMSGalentine_inact")**

Meg DiMartino
Digital Coordinator, EMILY's List

name: The White House
subject: No more roadblocks
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff122a0c692c16ee6f50b76a3854c0abdeae129eaa8af48ff3e4c1546ff7b60b37672a2c6d853903476cfacddee3f2d44f24e52a78a5ffc3

No more roadblocks to rebuilding America

The Anderson Memorial Bridge between Boston and Cambridge, Massachusetts, sits near the heart of Harvard University. An elegant, Georgian Revival structure, it took 11 months to build the original bridge in 1912.

When it came time to repair it nearly 100 years later, the project dragged on for close to 5 years—at a significant cost overrun.

Why did it take more than 5 times as long to repair the structure today as it did to create it more than a century ago? The Anderson Bridge project was the victim of a bloated, tangled patchwork of regulatory oversight that squanders the advantages of modern technology in building fast, safe, and reliable infrastructure throughout America.

This week, President Donald J. Trump laid out six principles for reversing this unacceptable trend. One such principle: Return decision-making authority to the state and local communities whose needs are most at stake.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff122a0c692c16ee8e63889b66264088b9f00f3e1fc7df89a9f30922fd3990ac2dc078135ec0fee139d7220b26cbcf19fbe19e8c0bdbe485
See the President’s 6 principles for rebuilding America here.

A special 200th birthday

Most people know February 14 as Valentine’s Day, but today holds another significance that all Americans should be familiar with: It is the 200th birthday of abolitionist leader Frederick Douglass.

In honor of African American History Month, Vice President Mike Pence visited the National Museum of African American History and Culture in Washington yesterday. Authorized by President George W. Bush in 2003 and opened to the public in 2016, the museum has quickly become a major destination in the Nation’s capital.

“It is deeply humbling for me to stand before you today in the midst of this great national monument to the struggles, the sacrifices, and the triumphs of so many American heroes,” the Vice President said. “The history that’s recorded in these halls literally is stitched into every fabric of the American story.”

Later yesterday afternoon, African American leaders from across the country joined President Trump and the First Lady for a reception at the White House commemorating African American History Month.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff122a0c692c16eefc4905fb4e4f0173f2a5490ae54e414a57e0c5392250d3c177734e509d328306335f00eea51033afcf889d45c72a815f
Read more about the special meaning of this year’s African American History Month.

On tax reform, the good news keeps rolling in

In the weeks following the passage of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, American companies have unleashed a remarkable stream of wage increases, bonuses, and retirement account contributions—all while sharing plans for even greater investment in workers and equipment alike.

As of this week, more than 350 companies have made these intentions public, announcing more than $150 billion in planned investments at home and $4.2 billion in bonuses paid to more than 4 million U.S. workers. The largest U.S. company, the largest U.S. bank, and the largest U.S. employer have all announced intentions to raise worker wages or give out bonuses during the past few weeks.

What does all of this good news add up to? While it’s impossible to measure with complete certainty the effects of such sweeping legislation, the President’s Council of Economic Advisers estimates that the total increase in annual pay resulting from tax reform will reach $4,000 per household.

Read more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff122a0c692c16eecfb96686c6dc3fa63a1a008608c0a9d7feaa42675969c5d0750a31c40db7d5abb1da5b7879f0f18166ddace897de8e7a
Companies announce benefits of tax reform for American workers

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump, First Lady Melania Trump, and U.S. Surgeon General Vice Adm. Jerome M. Adams | February 13, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump will meet with bipartisan members of Congress about infrastructure. In the afternoon, the President will participate in a working session regarding the opportunity zones provided by tax reform.

Later, the President will sign H.R. 4708, the DHS Blue Campaign Authorization Act, and S. 534, the Protecting Young Victims from Sexual Assault and Safe Sport Authorization Act.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff122a0c692c16eee601390728b1783390d62b2b4a899159d9f339bf20dd6b8b67578c611a2593409b4cb9e28dab5bc8e94e7ec9bd6bb3aa
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=ff122a0c692c16ee2236e912a2963c4d49ef793fe548d72f9f2d48ccc0a4b30cdd8014c0b5daba9743458c1dcbec0ccfa1f721717a48cf84
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d43c013a3e0ed6ee0a8b9eedee3b7b74a74578836ada89bbf342f302018be8444f8a92623ff78bd36398e7c6c5eba5e386377f60d0c1b346bf

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: They're coming after your Medicare
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | Late last year, Speaker Paul Ryan and Florida's very own Senator Marco Rubio admitted they want to come after Medicare and Social Security this year. So, how long do you think it will take them? Another month? Maybe two? Taking away much needed dollars from Medicare and Social Security is a "win" in their books. It's a disaster in mine. **And for seniors in Florida, the idea of losing their benefits is a flat-out nightmare.** I will not stop fighting to protect seniors. I need to know you're with me. [_**We have a big deadline at the end of this short month -- $20,000 by the 28th. Can you chip in to help us reach it?**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f87/622b7b5b/19788fd5/307bb504/2985207282/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse180220?refcode=dwse180220_a&recurring=true&amount=5") Attacking Medicare and Social Security seems like a favorite pastime of the Republican Party. With Trump in the White House, any one of their attacks could be successful this time around. There's one thing I can say: **cuts to Medicare or Social Security are unacceptable.** Any changes that threaten the financial stability of seniors are non-starters for me. Of course, my outspoken attitude doesn't make me a fan favorite with Republicans, President Trump, or their special interest allies. Which is fine by me - because I will not sit silent when these critical programs are threatened. **But I need you on my side on this.** It will take the strength of our entire grassroots network to push back against the anti-Medicare, anti-Social Security agenda. [_**I need you to come through this month. So if you can, chip in $5 or more.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f87/622b7b5b/19788fd5/307bb504/2985207282/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse180220?refcode=dwse180220_a&recurring=true&amount=5") Thank you. Debbie

name: Team Kaine
subject: The Tea Party Majority Fund is spending against Tim
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
It's starting: In January, the Tea Party Majority Fund officially invested $25,000 in our race - $25,000 that they said will go directly into anti-Tim Kaine phone calls to voters.

**Will you help us fight back against this outside money? Make a donation to Tim's re-election campaign today:**

[**https://timkaine.com/outside-money**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=jfeIsvprl88nalcAfWX9dPaz3c9ogNIa)

The Tea Party Majority Fund isn't the only group spending money against Tim - a PAC called "The Committee to Defend the President" has filed *fourteen* separate expenditures during this campaign cycle, according to the Federal Election Commission.

These FEC filings are public, which allows our team to keep track of the money flowing into this race to try to defeat Tim - meaning that on any given day, we can figure out what we need to do to fight back. Right now, fighting back means raising $25,000 to combat the money coming from the Tea Party Majority Fund.

**Can you make a contribution?** [**To chip in towards the $25,000 total, you can donate quickly and securely online right now.**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=qesEI5LuHdlGtz7tzBZVLvaz3c9ogNIa)

Thank you so much - we know that re-electing Tim is a critical part of standing up to an extreme administration under President Trump, and we appreciate everything you do to support this campaign.

\- Team Kaine

subject: Trump just had to drop this threat
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend, LegitAction members just helped achieve a major win
for democratic legitimacy!

Thomas Brunell, President Trump’s pro-gerrymandering pick for a top
spot at the U.S. Census Bureau, just withdrew from consideration --
mere weeks after thousands of Legit supporters signed petitions and
made calls to sound the alarm about Brunell.

Right now, we need to prepare to win the next fight. Because the
question isn’t if the administration will unleash another major threat
to our voting rights again this year -- the question is when.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/go/5474?t=1002&akid=620%2E356062%2EeMtg-O ]Help keep racking up these groundbreaking wins for legitimacy.
Donate
$3 so we can keep striking down
threats like Brunell’s appointment to the Census Bureau.

LegitAction’s efforts are making history this year. We helped end the
sham Trump-Pence election commission in January, and our rapid
response to Thomas Brunell’s candidacy just helped prevent a notorious
gerrymandering advocate from controlling the census. We’re making a
serious difference.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today, and every
day.

Russ Feingold

body:
| | | Hi friends -

Remember [the radio ad](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ad22f2b5dd&e=1acd9bb3d6) Chairman Hupfer emailed you about a few weeks ago? The one that asks why Democrat Joe Donnelly voted against cutting federal taxes for hardworking Hoosiers?

Well, we’ve got big news. Thanks to generous supporters like you who wanted to turn the heat up on Democrat Donnelly and [keep the ad on the air](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2195e27bf2&e=1acd9bb3d6), we are now on Rush Limbaugh! After running on stations across the state for two weeks, we are now on the airwaves of one of the highest-rated programs in Central Indiana.

If you haven't had a chance to listen to the ad yet, [take a listen now](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=966f2b2905&e=1acd9bb3d6). It features two real Hoosiers -- not actors -- talking about the real money they're both saving thanks to President Trump's tax cuts.

And, if you can, [please consider chipping in](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0eeacc86f9&e=1acd9bb3d6) to help keep this ad airing border-to-border across the Hoosier State!

Thanks,

Pete Seat
Executive Director of Strategic Communications & Talent Development
Indiana Republican Party

___

**From:** Chairman Kyle Hupfer
**Date:** Jan. 31, 2018 at 5:55:18 PM EST
**Subject:** Why Didn't Democrat Donnelly Tell the Truth?
---
| |
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b0d9d30e8d&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| | Friends -

Last night at his State of the Union address, President Donald Trump said it best. With the president's tax cut putting more money into Hoosier pockets, this truly is our American moment -- it's time to purse the American Dream!

**But not if you ask Democrat Joe Donnelly.** That's because he _voted against these historic reforms,_ which will place an _additional $1,550/year_ into the pockets of your average Hoosier.

Earlier today, our team at your Indiana Republican Party [debuted a new radio advertisement](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0a622a7c43&e=1acd9bb3d6) asking why Democrat Donnelly said **__"NO"__** when asked if he thought you should get to keep more of your hard-earned money.
[**> >Listen NOW to our new radio ad <<**](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=07902bd8f0&e=1acd9bb3d6)
[The ad launched today](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ddafb928cf&e=1acd9bb3d6) in Central Indiana, and will air **in all of Indiana's 92 counties** **starting this Monday**. **_But that's where we need your help._**

We've put five-figures behind this ad, but sharing this message isn't cheap, and [we need your help](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b207c4af08&e=1acd9bb3d6) making sure we have the funds to keep this ad airing.

These aren't actors, **_they're real Hoosiers_** , and they each report that they'll see __larger paychecks__ thanks to President Trump and Hoosier Republicans -- **and no thanks to Democrat Joe Donnelly.**

In fact, Kelly's family of two adults and four children will save _$2,800/year_ , while Matt and his wife will _save $1,000/year_. **And it's the same story for everyday Hoosiers across our state.**

Of course, if you listened to Democrat Donnelly in December, you heard him say that this tax cut would only benefit "the wealthiest Americans, while raising taxes on a majority of families making less than $75,000."

And now -- with stories of Hoosiers like Kelly and Matt being told across the state -- **we know that Donnelly was flat wrong.**

**__Why didn't Democrat Donnelly tell the truth? What's in it for him?__**

With Hoosiers going to the polls in just a few short months, they need to know that **Democrat Donnelly voted against cutting your taxes**. As Hoosiers see their paychecks rise in the coming weeks, they need to be asking these important questions.

Help us elect a Republican to the U.S. Senate in November who stands with President Trump, and who will vote to _cut our taxes_ and _energize our American economy_ , helping us each pursue our version of the American Dream. _[Help us share this important message today.](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=719738dd98&e=1acd9bb3d6)_

Thanks,
Kyle Hupfer
Chairman, Indiana Republican Party

body:
Let me tell you a few things you need to know about the budget Donald Trump proposed this week >>
---
[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d581/2b07114c/8d7865df/731cc1f0/1037238856/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/fight-safety-net-cuts?source=PT-20180214-Nielsen-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
Team,

Let me tell you a few things you need to know about the budget Donald Trump proposed this week:

* It breaks Trump's promise never to cut Medicare or Medicaid, with billions of dollars in devastating cuts to both programs;

* It would end student loan forgiveness;

* And it would replace food stamps for millions of families with boxes of canned goods.

That's just the starting point. This budget is an all-out assault on our social safety net and would be utterly catastrophic for millions of working Americans.

[**We have to fight back immediately. Add your name: Say you oppose Trump 's budget and massive safety net cuts.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d581/2b07114c/8d7865df/731cc1f0/1037238856/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/fight-safety-net-cuts?source=PT-20180214-Nielsen-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Here's what makes me angriest: Republicans just gave billionaires and major corporations a totally unnecessary $1.5 trillion tax cut...and now they're trying to pay for it on the backs of working families.

As _The_ _Washington Post_ wrote, "Trump's budget hits poor Americans the hardest." It's unconscionable that the GOP would try to slash funds that guarantee food, housing and medical care to our most vulnerable Americans -- and especially that they'd choose to give the already-wealthy a tax break instead.

**Act now before these cuts are final. Add your name immediately: Tell Trump and his fellow Republicans NO cuts to the safety net:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/stop-safety-net-cuts**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d581/2b07114c/8d7865df/731cc1f0/1037238856/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/fight-safety-net-cuts?source=PT-20180214-Nielsen-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Thank you,

Stephanie Nielsen
DSCC Research Director

**A quick note on donations, team:**

We have to show the GOP they'll face a wall of resistance if they come after crucial safety net programs. A full 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We don't have the Koch brothers in our corner -- we have you. And your contribution right now will show you're standing strong with Senate Democrats as they fight to protect the safety net and propel our candidates to victory.

[**Contribute**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democrats?refcode=DPE-20180214-Nielsen-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d581/2b07114c/8d7865df/731cc1ec/1037238856/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). | | Friends, The results are in: the Republican-controlled state legislature handed in failing work on their assignment to produce a _fair_ map of Pennsylvania's congressional districts. The state Supreme Court told them their previous maps were unconstitutional, but Pennsylvania Republicans couldn't help themselves. **Their new map is just as ridiculous as their old map.** And to make matters worse? _Once again_ they did it behind closed doors with _zero_ input from the public or their Democratic colleagues. Typical. Republican disregard for the rule of law is an infection from top to bottom, from Donald Trump to right here in Pennsylvania. The Republican persistence in gerrymandering voting districts is an insult to democracy, to fairness, and to every person living in Pennsylvania. **We deserve better. That's why I wholeheartedly agree with Governor Tom Wolf's rejection of the map.** The process now goes on to the state Supreme Court. name: Donna Edwards
subject: We deserve to have a voice
body:
[](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff7e/767134fb/5cd48af2/43382eb6/3563937949/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=rtw_180213_schoolboardnatl_em&recurring=true")
Friends,
In most places, when you send your child to public school, you're putting your child's future in the hands of someone you've had a say in hiring.
But in Prince George's County, parents and teachers have no say in who's making the decisions that affect our children -- we have a school board that is appointed, rather than elected.
**[Shouldn't parents have a voice in this process? Make a $25 contribution now to help me advocate for an elected school board that parents and teachers can hold accountable.](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff7e/767134fb/5cd48af2/43382eb6/3563937949/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=rtw_180213_schoolboardnatl_em&recurring=true")**
As Prince George's County Executive, I will advocate for a transition to an elected school board so that parents and teachers have a say in the decisions affecting our children.
We should be investing in our children's education and providing our teachers with enough resources to give each child the individual attention they need to learn, grow, and thrive.
But the unelected and unaccountable school board wants to close schools and pack more students into classrooms that are already bursting at the seams. From grade changes to graduation rates to how your taxes are spent, you deserve answers.
Without parents and teachers being a part of the conversation, the unelected school board can make decisions about our children's futures without any accountability.
**[Will you make a $25 contribution to my campaign now so I can continue to advocate for an accountable, elected school board in Prince George's County?](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff7e/767134fb/5cd48af2/43382eb6/3563937949/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-email?refcode=rtw_180213_schoolboardnatl_em&recurring=true")**
We have to protect our children and our future,
Donna

subject: The 11 Democratic women of the Senate need your support, friend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
The Democratic women of the Senate are like the U.S. Olympic team: They may all be different in their approach but they're all united in their determination to do what's best for our country, for our fellow Americans.
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180214-Mikulski-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Friend --

The Democratic women of the Senate are like the U.S. Olympic team: They may all be different in their approach but they're all united in their determination to do what's best for our country, for our fellow Americans.

They're champions for creating jobs, making quality childcare more affordable, and strengthening the middle class -- and that's why we need to make sure we re-elect them this November.

Friend, HALF the women of the Senate are up for re-election this year -- and we can't afford to lose a single one. We also have the wonderful opportunity of adding two more female champions (and flipping two red seats to blue!) with Jacky Rosen in Nevada and Kyrsten Sinema in Arizona.

[**So I have to ask: Can I count on you today to help get these women elected? The DSCC 's Women's Senate Network is committed to getting each of our Democratic women Senate candidates elected this November -- and your support to them today will go a long way. Simply chip in $1 to show your support >>**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180214-Mikulski-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

The fact is that when women are in positions of power, our national debate reflects the needs and dreams of American families -- from affordable healthcare to quality education.

But more importantly, it means women know how to work to get the job done.

Friend, our Democratic women understand the needs of working Americans -- and you bet they'll continue to fight and do what's best for American families.

[**Now, let 's make sure we keep them in the Senate. Chip in today to support the DSCC's Women's Senate Network and help our 13 Democratic women candidates >>**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180214-Mikulski-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Barbara Mikulski

clean_body: Derek, Republicans have heart. Show us yours! For Valentine's Day, we want to showcase just how much New Hampshire Republicans love New Hampshire. We know that members of the New Hampshire Republican Party love the Granite State, and the New Hampshire GOP wants to shine a spotlight on the good works you do in your community. Many of you show love for your community by volunteering in one of the hundreds of New Hampshire non-profits. Tell us your story - What nonprofits are you involved with? Why do you volunteer? Go to nh.gop/lovenh to submit your #GOPLoveNH story, and the Party will highlight you on social media in the next two weeks!
